config.exam_name: 44 Databricks SQL Alerts

question.line: What is the primary purpose of Databricks SQL Alerts?

options.A: To automatically optimize SQL queries for better performance
options.B: To continuously monitor SQL query results and send notifications when specified conditions are met
options.C: To automatically create Materialized Views from SQL queries
options.D: To replicate SQL query results into Delta Lake
options.E: To schedule notebook execution in Databricks Workflows

question.line: Which of the following is a common use case for Databricks SQL Alerts?

options.B: Monitoring pending order counts, data quality issues, and business KPIs
options.C: Converting CSV files into Delta tables

question.line: Every Databricks SQL Alert is based on which of the following?
Which component executes the SQL query used by a Databricks SQL Alert?

options.B: SQL Warehouse attached to the saved query

question.line: Which comparison operator is supported when configuring a SQL Alert trigger condition?
If a SQL Alert is configured with the condition Pending Orders > 10,000 and the query returns 191,189, what will happen?

options.C: The alert status becomes Triggered because the condition evaluates to TRUE.
options.E: The SQL Warehouse is stopped automatically.

question.line: What notification behavior helps prevent repeated alerts while the monitored condition remains true?

options.B: Trigger once until the condition returns to normal
options.C: Run the SQL query continuously without evaluation
options.D: Disable notifications after the first query execution
options.E: Execute a full refresh before every evaluation

question.line: Which of the following is NOT a supported notification destination for Databricks SQL Alerts?
Who can create notification destinations such as Email, Slack, or Microsoft Teams in Databricks?
What is the difference between a Query Schedule and an Alert Schedule?

options.A: They are the same schedule managed together.
options.B: The Query Schedule refreshes dashboards, while the Alert Schedule refreshes Materialized Views.
options.C: The Query Schedule executes the saved query, while the Alert Schedule determines when the alert condition is evaluated.
options.D: The Query Schedule only works with notebooks, while the Alert Schedule only works with SQL Warehouses.
options.E: The Query Schedule creates notifications, while the Alert Schedule creates SQL queries.
